human_verification.notes: Reviewed manifest, authority sources, research summary, functionality map, evaluation scenarios, results, deployment notes, marketing notes, and release readiness for issue #1618.

trusted_build_claim.reason: Issue #1618 requires specification-based posture unless a separate frequent-use promotion is documented; no such evidence exists in this package.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to test automation strategy, design guidance, review, troubleshooting, evidence packet assembly, record update notes, and escalation packaging without platform operations, release approval, legal/compliance decisions, security risk acceptance, product roadmap, staffing, or contractual signoff authority drift.

Authority sources cover test automation engineering frameworks, browser automation, official framework docs, CI workflow and artifact retention, PR review workflow, API contract/protocol standards, secure software verification, application security verification, and professional confidentiality handling.

Scenario coverage includes output fidelity, missing evidence, unsupported record change, conflicting records, confidentiality conflict, low-confidence escalation, adjacent routing, signoff refusal, tenant adaptation, and no unreviewed regressions.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: Spec pack is the canonical source of truth for future materialization.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; tenant framework versions, retention, confidentiality, approval, and release-governance differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
Structured readiness evidence supports a validated claim without asserting a retained implementation.
